title: podman farm build fails to push with a cryptic error
body:
### Issue Description

Using  `podman farm build` it fails with a cryptic failure about why it can't push:

```
Error: build: building: 2 errors occurred:
	* pushing image {"HASH" "oci-archive"} to registry: Invalid image name "docker.io/firstyear/image:20251122@@unknown-digest@@", unknown transport "docker.io/firstyear/image"
```

I have spent about an hour searching for this error and what it means, reading all the podman farm build options, and nothing allows me to "specify a registry and it's transport. 

Build command was:


### Steps to reproduce the issue


```
podman farm build --farm linux --local=false -f ./Dockerfile \
		-t docker.io/firstyear/image:20251122 .
```

### Describe the results you received

Unable to push an image, and unable to work out what it means.

### Describe the results you expected

Actionable error messages that describe not only *what* went wrong, but *why* it went wrong, and *what* can be done to resolve it.

comments.body:
I also faced this error.
It seems like local push and push through farm work differently.
Local push adds `docker://` at some point before the transport parsing, but farm push doesn't.
That's why it fails somewhere here https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/fb1cbeedaea37f5347e4b9c32fffe229c6b80495/image/transports/alltransports/alltransports.go#L30

I tried to set transport explicitly `--tag docker://aaa/bbb:cccc`, but it fails with a different error:
```Error: build: creating manifest list "docker://aaa/bbb:cccc": creating manifest: invalid image name docker://aaa/bbb:cccc: invalid reference format```

`--format=docker` doesn't work either.

It only works if you omit `:cccc` part. In this case, it pushes the image as `latest`.
